description_long: This is a wooden barrel with a classic cylindrical shape, slightly bulging at the center and tapering towards the top and bottom. The exterior is constructed from vertical wooden staves, bound together by dark, thin bands that provide structural reinforcement. The surface of the barrel is stained with dark red liquid, which appears to have dripped and pooled in several places, suggesting it may have contained wine or another colored fluid. The top of the barrel is flat and also shows evidence of liquid spillage, with some stains running down the sides. The wood grain is visible throughout, giving the object a rustic and aged appearance. There are no visible handles or labeling, and the lid appears to be firmly in place.
description: A wooden barrel with dark bands and red liquid stains running down its sides, featuring a flat, closed top and a rustic, aged appearance.
description_view_0: Front view shows the most prominent red liquid stains dripping from the top and pooling on the lid.
description_view_1: This side reveals fewer stains, with some drips running down the staves and a clearer view of the wood grain.
description_view_2: This angle highlights a large central stain and several long drips, emphasizing the barrel's bulging midsection.
description_view_3: This view shows two distinct stains near the top edge and a relatively clean lower section.
